title: Suzuka Circuit
sub: The only figure-eight on the calendar and a driver’s favourite. Flowing esses, the daunting 130R, and a rhythm that punishes the smallest hesitation.
mapCredit: Will Pittenger / Wikimedia Commons · CC BY-SA

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Turn 1-2 into the iconic uphill Esses (T3-7). Commitment and rhythm: the heart of a Suzuka lap.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The Degner curves, the hairpin, the long right to Spoon. Technical, varied and tyre-punishing.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The back straight, the fearsome flat-out 130R, and the final chicane onto the main straight.

tyreAlloc: Hardest in the range · C1 / C2 / C3
stratNote: Continuous high-speed direction changes (esses, 130R) put massive lateral energy through the tyres. New asphalt has reduced wear from the old two-stop norm.
spots: End of the main straight + the lone DRS zone. Primary passing chance.
Heavy braking after 130R; a late lunge into the final chicane.
Occasional move on exit setting up the back straight.
otNote: A narrow, old-school track where dirty air through the fast esses makes following hard. Overtaking is tough; qualifying and tyre offset do the work.
corners: Fast right off the straight tightening into Turn 2; main braking + overtaking zone.
First half of the legendary uphill left-right sequence.
Continuing flick of direction; rhythm is everything.
Long left at the top of the esses onto the run to Degner.
Two quick rights; the second a notorious snap-oversteer trap.
Under the crossover bridge, into the slow section.
Tight, slow hairpin; traction and a possible lunge on entry.
Quick right building speed toward Spoon.
A long, tightening double-apex left; exit feeds the back straight.
Long straight where slipstreams build for the chicane.
Near-flat left at ~290 km/h. One of the great corners in the sport.
Heavy braking right-left; last real overtaking chance.
geo: The lone circuit that crosses over itself, the back section passing under the main straight on a bridge. A Honda test track from 1962.
Turns 3-7 are a flowing, uphill sequence at huge speed. Rhythm and commitment define a lap.
A near-flat-out left at ~290 km/h, one of the most fearsome corners in motorsport.
Held in spring, Suzuka often serves up rain and wind off the coast, on a low-grip, unforgiving surface.
